antipattern.symptom: Linear fusion configured; BM25 scores dominate; vector signal contributes near-zero; results barely differ from BM25-only.
antipattern.root-cause: Linear combination applied without normalising BM25 (unbounded) and vector (bounded ~0-1) scores. BM25 magnitudes swamp the linear sum.
antipattern.fix: Switch to RRF (rank-only, no normalisation) OR add min-max normalisation per signal before linear combination.

antipattern.symptom: Reranker enabled; precision uplift is < 1 point; latency up 80 ms.
antipattern.root-cause: Reranker fed only top-10 fusion candidates; nothing for it to recover.
antipattern.fix: Fetch top-100 from fusion; rerank to top-10. If precision still insufficient, increase candidate pool to 200.

antipattern.symptom: Hybrid wins on natural-language eval queries but loses on product-code queries vs BM25-only.
antipattern.root-cause: Single global alpha (e.g. 0.7) tuned for natural language penalises exact-match queries.
antipattern.fix: Apply query-adaptive alpha schedule (quoted=0.2, code=0.3, short=0.5, default=0.7). Re-bench against both query types.

antipattern.symptom: App layer issues two requests (BM25 + vector) and fuses in Python; latency = sum of two calls; one slow node stalls the request.
antipattern.root-cause: Team unfamiliar with native FusionQuery / hybrid endpoints.
antipattern.fix: Use the vendor's native hybrid endpoint; single network call; vendor does fusion server-side.
